title: Spoilt Sport
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:SpoiltSport-SW1977-22.jpg]] |name=''Spoilt Sport'' |designer= |manufacturer= |line= |model=[[Yacht/Legends|Yacht]][[Star Wars (1977) 22|''Star Wars'' (1977) 22]] |type= |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem= |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen= |hull= |sensor= |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament= |complement= |bays= |escapepods= |crew= |skeleton= |passengers= |capacity= |cargohandling= |consumables= |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems= |role= |commission= |destroyed= |retired= |battles= |affiliation= |navy= |fleet= |owners=[[Simon Greyshade/Legends|Simon Greyshade]] |captains= |crewmembers= |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|That ship your gallant young friend seems bent on conquering is my private yacht!|Simon Greyshade, to Leia Organa|}} The '''''Spoilt Sport''''' was the personal [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]] of [[Simon Greyshade/Legends|Simon Greyshade]], the [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] of the [[Casino/Legends|casino]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as [[The Wheel/Legends|the ''Wheel'']]. Flashy and expensive, the ''Spoilt Sport'' was kept aboard the station and was linked into the Wheel's communications array. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Greyshade was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during a battle between [[Wheel Security Force|Wheel Security]] and [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], and control of the yacht fell to [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel]] agents [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], who used it to escape Imperial pursuit. They soon brought the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to [[Centares/Legends|Centares]], where they traded it for a more discreet craft. ==Description== {{Quote|This ship's not so fancy as that yacht you came in, but—
"'But it's got the drive and armament needed in the outerworlds!' We heard the sales pitch.|Jorman Thoad and Luke Skywalker, after the latter traded in the ''Spoilt Sport''|[[Star Wars (1977) 25|''Star Wars'' (1977) 25]]}} [[File:SpoiltSport.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The ''Spoilt Sport'' from above.]] A gleaming, golden [[Yacht/Legends|yacht]], the ''Spoilt Sport'' was the personal [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Simon Greyshade/Legends|Simon Greyshade]] during his [[Standard year/Legends|years]] as the [[Administrator/Legends|Administrator]] of the [[Casino/Legends|casino]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as [[The Wheel/Legends|the Wheel]]. Greyshade went through lengths to make sure that he would be the only one able to use his prized yacht, configuring the ship's control locks to only respond to his hand-print. Greyshade kept the ''Spoilt Sport'' in [[Hangar/Legends|Docking Bay]] 10 on the Wheel, where it was linked into the station's communication systems, allowing him to make [[Hologram/Legends|holo-calls]] to individuals aboard the Wheel while he stayed on his ship.[[Star Wars (1977) 23|''Star Wars'' (1977) 23]] ==History== {{Quote|This ship can take us to places in the galaxy where they've never heard of the Empire... or the Rebel Alliance!|Simon Greyshade, to Leia Organa|}} The ''Spoilt Sport'' belonged to Wheel Administrator Simon Greyshade during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], when the station became the center of intrigue concerning the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Imperial [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Zertik Strom/Legends|Zertik Strom]], looking to justify a direct Imperial takeover of the Wheel, staged a Rebel attack on a merchant ship carrying profits off the station. Although the Strom and Greyshade had struck a deal to allow the Imperial plot to move forward, Greyshade sent a squad of [[Wheel Security Force|Wheel Security]] officers to raid Strom's [[Enforcement (Pursuit-class)|cruiser]] and stash the stolen profits aboard the ''Spoilt Sport''. When Rebel [[Prison/Legends|prisoners]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], hoping to expose their scheme, attempted to track the security team back to the yacht, it resulted in a heated firefight. Skywalker attempted to take on the officers singlehandedly, but his charge up the ''Spoilt Sport''{{'s}} loading ramp was halted when Greyshade surprised him with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] in hand. [[File:SportDestroyer.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''Spoilt Sport'' attempts to evade Darth Vader's Star Destroyer.]] Greyshade brought Skywalker and Organa aboard the ''Spoilt Sport'' to commence negotiations, and the Wheel administrator offered generous terms. He would allow the Rebels to proceed in exposing Strom's scheme and allow Organa's friends to go free, asking only for one thing in return: for Organa, a former colleague in the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]] with whom he had long been infatuated, to take off with him in his yacht to parts unknown. After witnessing her close friend [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] seemingly perish in the Wheel's [[The Big Game|Big Game]] [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] contest, Organa reluctantly agreed to the deal. Before Greyshade and Organa could take off in the ''Spoilt Sport'', however, Strom declared the station under martial law and sent his [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] after Greyshade—he had learned of the administrator's treachery. Greyshade immediately disembarked the ship and prepared for the Imperials to storm Docking Bay 10, allowing Skywalker and Organa to escape on the ''Spoilt Sport''. Both Greyshade and Strom were killed in the ensuing firefight. Although the two Rebels had escaped the Wheel in Greyshade's yacht, they weren't home free: they were soon met by a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] under the command of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who had come to the Wheel in search of Skywalker. However, they were saved by the timely arrival of Solo and his [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], which attacked the [[Battleship/Legends|battleship]] and allowed the ''Spoilt Sport'' to slip away. Skywalker and Organa promptly brought the yacht to [[Centares/Legends|Centares]], where they traded it to local starship dealer [[Jorman Thoad]] for a less-flashy, more dependable [[Luke Skywalker's scout craft|scout craft]]. After Greyshade's death, the station was taken over by Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Mulchive Wermis]], who reigned as Administrator for several years until the Wheel was liberated by the Rebels. From there, control passed to [[Master-Com]], an ambulatory [[supercomputer]] that had been Greyshade's right hand. Master-Com served as Administrator for 97 years, until one day the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] disappeared in [[101 ABY]]. Rumors flew wildly regarding Master-Com's sudden departure, including one that one of its interchangeable droid bodies had been spotted [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] the antique ''Spoilt Sport'' towards a supposed "[[Kligson's Moon/Legends|Droid World]]."{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-2|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} ==Behind the scenes== The ''Spoilt Sport'' originally appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 22|''Star Wars'' (1977) 22]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1979]], During its comic appearances, the ship was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]]. Although the ship was originally unidentified, its name was finally given over 30 years later in [[Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel]], a pair of [[2013]] [[StarWars.com]] reference articles written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Rich Handley]].{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-1|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 22|''Star Wars'' (1977) 22]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 22}} {{1st}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 23|''Star Wars'' (1977) 23]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 23}} *[[Star Wars (1977) 25|''Star Wars'' (1977) 25]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 25}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-1|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} {{1stID|''Spoilt Sport''}} *{{SW|url=news/viva-space-vegas-the-history-of-the-marvelous-wheel-part-2|series=Viva Space Vegas! The History of the Marvelous Wheel|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Yachts]]
